self_bootstrap.method: spawned a minimal subagent via the Agent tool, captured its agentId from the tool result, then located its transcript by that id
self_bootstrap.note: the probe subagent declined the introspection task (overcautious refusal), but the Agent tool result still surfaced its agentId, which is all the bootstrap needs

join_validation.claim: agentId extracted from the filename == agentId carried in the transcript records == agentId from the spawn result (the join M-001/DL-016/DL-020 rely on)

transcript_format.note: every record carries both agentId and sessionId, so the path<->payload join can be reconstructed from any single line

cleanup_period_days.source: Claude Code settings `cleanupPeriodDays` default; not set in ~/.claude/settings.json or settings.local.json in this environment, so the 30-day default applies
cleanup_period_days.governs_subagent_transcripts: assumed (medium confidence) -- the setting governs ~/.claude/projects transcript retention; subagent transcripts live under that tree so are presumed covered

send_message.superseded_reason: DL-031: the original 'available:true' claim rested only on a result-string scrape (the Agent-tool result text mentioning SendMessage), which is advertisement, not an invocation outcome. That weak-evidence claim is withdrawn here.
send_message.resolved_verdict: INCONCLUSIVE_NONINTERACTIVE -> defer to the official doc (SendMessage gated behind CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1)
send_message.note: Operational risk is LOW: the substrate does not depend on SendMessage (DL-009 resume re-invokes the entry point). See send_message_gate_probe_result.json for the per-trial record and interactive re-test instructions.

permission_mode.plan_spawn_note: the Agent tool and Workflow agent() opts do not expose permissionMode directly; permissionMode lives on subagent .md frontmatter (DL-017/DL-023), so a direct plan-mode spawn was not exercised in this probe. Tagged medium confidence.

parent_precedence.claim_DL_021: a parent session running bypassPermissions/acceptEdits/auto overrides a child's permissionMode
parent_precedence.implication: in THIS user's configuration the parent mode is `auto`, one of the overriding modes -> classify_phases must default-deny (auto_replay nothing) and /resume must warn that permissionMode enforcement is overridden (DL-021/R-007). This is a live, not hypothetical, condition here.

session_scoped_resume_restatement: Native subagent transcripts are session-scoped: they live under {sessionId}/ and are read by resumeFromRunId only within the same session. Cross-session, workflow-level resume is NOT provided by the native runtime and still requires the durable substrate (DL-001).
fallback_when_path_not_found: Per DL-020: if the native transcript path cannot be resolved from the run's stored session_id + native_agent_id, is_resumable() returns False (assume cleaned) rather than falling back to a started_at proxy.
